reasoning: The question asks whether there will be more than 10 times the average number of 'Battles' in Kenya within a 30-day period. The baseline reference value is ~13.75, which means the required threshold is >137.5 battles within 30 days (about 4.6 battles per day). ACLED defines 'Battles' strictly as violent interactions between two organized armed groups (excluding riots and protests). Historically, a 10x spike in a single month requires the outbreak of a massive civil war or invasion. Recent search results from mid-to-late 2025 indicate major political protests and some border clashes with Al-Shabaab, but nothing remotely close to a full-scale armed insurgency that would generate 138 battles in a month. Because the first resolution date is only a week away and most of the 30-day window has already elapsed peacefully at normal levels, the probability is near rock-bottom. Distant resolution dates receive marginally higher probabilities to account for unknown future instability, but remain extremely low due to the strict required conditions of a massive, sudden civil war.
